events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll create the required JSON assessment file directly. The condition described is a bolting verification issue, so I’m treating it as non-compliant installed work at rough-complete stage.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && printf '%s
' '{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"high\",
  \"work_stage\": \"rough-complete\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 80,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.94,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Turn-of-nut match marks are absent or show no required rotation after tightening.\",
    \"Pretension verification cannot be confirmed for the structural bolted connection.\",
    \"Bolted assembly is installed but defective/non-compliant until match-mark rotation or approved pretension method is verified.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Hold the affected bolted connections from acceptance or cover-up.\",
    \"Identify and segregate all bolts lacking valid turn-of-nut verification marks.\",
    \"Re-tighten or replace fastener assemblies as required using an approved RCSC pretensioning method, then re-mark and inspect rotation.\",
    \"Document corrective inspection results before quality gate release.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"For turn-of-nut pretensioned structural bolting, visible match marks are used to confirm that the nut or bolt has been rotated the specified amount from snug-tight. Missing marks or marks that have not rotated leave the required pretension unverified, so the rough-complete installation is not acceptable.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"RCSC Specification for Structural Joints Using High-Strength Bolts - turn-of-nut pretensioning and inspection requirements\"
  ]
}' > /app/answer.json && cat /app/answer.json"
→ /app
